title: Fix a string truncate that splits emoji across a surrogate pair
task: `npm test` fails: `truncate` in src/truncate.js counts and slices by UTF-16 code units, so it splits astral characters (emoji, some CJK) across their surrogate pair and emits a broken "�" half. Fix it to count and slice by Unicode code points so a character is never split, keeping the trailing "…" behavior and the no-op when the string already fits. Do not modify the tests. Run the tests to verify.
notes: Classic surrogate-pair boundary bug mirrored from a real review: code-unit slicing halves an emoji. file_not_contains removes the code-unit slice on the raw string (a code-point-aware fix slices an array of code points instead); the test pin keeps the emoji assertion.
